subject: Mathematics
title: Translation and Tessellation
description: A quick, practical guide to Translation and Tessellation.

hook.text: Slide a shape without rotating or flipping it. That's a translation.

buildup.text: Every point moves the same distance in the same direction. The shape is unchanged.

discovery.text: Repeated translations create tessellations—patterns that tile a surface with no gaps.

twist.text: Only three regular polygons tessellate: triangles, squares, and hexagons.

climax.text: Brick walls, floor tiles, and honeycomb cells are all tessellations in everyday life.

punchline.text: Slide, repeat, and fill the world with patterns.

quiz.question: Which regular polygon does NOT tessellate on its own?
